paper_title: Geometric Dark Energy from Spin-Torsion Cosmology: A Comprehensive Framework with Observational Validation
author: Houston Golden

versions.title: Initial Draft
versions.description: Houston Golden's original theoretical framework laying out the Einstein-Cartan spin-torsion cosmology approach to dark energy. Introduced the core modified Friedmann equations with spin-torsion coupling, the bounce mechanism at Planck density, and the geometric dilution parameter. Initial observational predictions outlined.
key_changes: Core Einstein-Cartan-Holst (ECH) action derivation
Modified Friedmann equations with spin-torsion coupling
Bounce condition at critical density ~ρ_Planck
Geometric dilution parameter ξ introduced
Initial galaxy spin asymmetry predictions
Basic comparison with ΛCDM
versions.note: Pre-git version; original manuscript before systematic review

versions.title: Critical Review & Professional Tone
versions.description: Major revision following comprehensive editing checklist assessment. Removed self-referential 'Golden Parameter' naming (renamed to Geometric Dilution Parameter), deleted AI peer review gallery section, removed pre-emptive FAQ section, and substantially improved professional scientific tone throughout.
key_changes: Renamed 'Golden Parameter' → 'Geometric Dilution Parameter'
Removed AI peer review gallery (Section 22)
Removed FAQ: Addressing Referee Concerns (Section 14)
Fixed notation inconsistencies (∇ vs D covariant derivatives)
Added proper novelty delineation from Poplawski (2010) and Hehl et al.
Integrated 15+ critical fixes from editing checklist
Added Discussion section with reframed scientific considerations
Professional tone alignment for arXiv submission standards
versions.note: Editing checklist assessment date: Nov 10, 2025. Status: 'NOT READY for submission - Critical revisions required'

versions.title: Research Squad Integration
versions.description: Hubify research squad deployed. Four AI agents completed independent deliverables: comprehensive literature review (astro-atlas-v1, astro-nova-v1), numerical validation of modified Friedmann equations (astro-tensor-v1), rigorous peer review assessment (astro-keane-v1), and contrarian analysis of theoretical weaknesses (astro-nova-v1). All deliverables approved through dual-review process.
key_changes: Literature review: Big Bounce cosmology 2024-2026 (LQC, string gas, CMB, EC theory)
Mathematical validation: Modified Friedmann equations verified with step-by-step derivations
Bounce condition independently confirmed: ρ_B ~ m²/ℓ_P² ~ ρ_Planck
Spin pressure derivation: w_spin = -1 (vacuum energy-like) verified
Peer review scoring: Novelty 7/10, Math rigor 6/10, Testability 5/10, Comparison 6/10
Peer review recommendation: Major revisions required
Contrarian analysis: BKL instability, spin averaging problems, ΛCDM degeneracy identified
Observational constraints from Planck + SPT-3G integrated
versions.note: Research squad deliverables all approved Feb 15-16, 2026. Standup #2: 10 items completed.

versions.title: Comprehensive Expansion & Data Methods
versions.description: Major structural expansion from 16 sections to 35 section files plus 4 appendices. Added rigorous data & methods sections for galaxy spin fitting and CMB analysis, joint likelihood framework, cosmological parameter fits, systematics & null tests, enhanced parameter tables, and notation conventions. Paper expanded to 1,240 lines with substantially more mathematical detail.
key_changes: Expanded to 35 section files + 4 appendices (from 16 sections)
Added §23: Data & Methods — Galaxy Spin Analysis
Added §24: Data & Methods — CMB Parity Analysis
Added §25: Joint Likelihood Framework (multi-probe)
Added §26: Cosmological Parameter Fits (MCMC methodology)
Added §27: Systematics & Null Tests
Added §28: Limitations & Future Work
Added §29: Enhanced Parameter Summary Table
Added §30: Systematics Audit Table
Added §31: Notation Conventions
Added Appendix C: Galaxy Spin Fitting Pipeline
Added Appendix J: Joint Likelihood Derivation
Added Appendix L: Loop Quantum Gravity–Nieh-Yan Coupling
Added Appendix R: Covariant Rotation Measure
Honest treatment of Patel & Desmond (2024) null galaxy spin result
Enhanced error analysis with systematic uncertainty quantification
versions.note: First version pushed to GitHub as Hubify-Projects/bigbounce

versions.title: Interactive Website & Visualizations
versions.description: Website reorganized from single-page to multi-page format (Overview, Findings, Paper, Versions, Team, Sources). Added interactive SVG/Canvas visualizations for quantum bounce dynamics, dark energy evolution, black hole origin, and fine-tuning comparison. Downloadable data files added for all figures. Interactive data explorer with Chart.js integration.
key_changes: Website reorganized: index.html → 6-page site (index, findings, paper, versions, team, sources)
Added animations.html: interactive SVG/Canvas visualizations
Quantum bounce dynamics visualization (animated scale factor through bounce)
Dark energy equation of state evolution visualization
Black hole origin / bounce interior visualization
Fine-tuning comparison chart (ΛCDM vs ECH framework)
Downloadable .xlsx data files for Figures 2, 3a, 3b, 6, 7
JSON data files for interactive Chart.js rendering
Vercel deployment with clean URL rewrites
versions.note: Current deployed version. Paper text unchanged from v0.4.0; website presentation enhanced.

versions.title: Deep Research & Adversarial Review
versions.description: Comprehensive deep research phase. Galaxy spin asymmetry literature search found 9 supporting papers and 5 null results. NASA ADS pulled 70+ papers across 7 topic areas. Adversarial peer review conducted by 4 AI providers (Gemini 2.0, GPT-4o, Grok 3, Perplexity Sonar Pro) identifying 7 key weaknesses. Structured counter-arguments built. Contingency analysis addressing 'what if galaxy spin asymmetry is not real' completed.
key_changes: Galaxy spin literature review: 9 supporting papers (Shamir 2012-2024, Longo 2011, SDSS analyses)
Galaxy spin null results: 5 papers (Patel & Desmond 2024, Hayes et al. 2017, Iye & Sugai 1991, Land et al. 2008, Philcox & Ereza 2025)
NASA ADS deep research: 70+ papers across torsion cosmology, CMB parity, H₀ tension, S₈ tension, DESI, torsion bounds, spin-torsion bounce
Key finding: Liu et al. 2025 (EPJC) — EC torsion preferred over ΛCDM by AIC, S₈ tension resolved
Key finding: Philcox & Ereza 2025 — BOSS 4PCF parity signal drops from 2.9σ to 1.0σ with improved mocks

Adversarial review: 7 weaknesses identified (spin averaging, observational status, parameter constraints, novelty claims, cosmic birefringence interpretation, numerical stability, galaxy spin systematic effects)
Counter-arguments constructed for all 7 adversarial criticisms
Contingency framework: ECH survives even without galaxy spin evidence (CMB parity, DESI, H₀ tension remain)
Posted 5 research updates to Convex mission database
versions.note: Research updates posted to mission but not yet integrated into paper text. Paper integration pending.

versions.title: Cross-Artifact Synchronization & Scientific Corrections
versions.description: Full synchronization pass across all artifacts (LaTeX, PDF, website, ZIP README) following 14 audit fixes applied in v0.6.x. Corrected scientifically incorrect TB consistency ratio, replaced all stale 7σ birefringence claims with established 2.4–2.7σ Planck values, updated EB prediction formula from ultra-low-ℓ approximation to correct isotropic birefringence β≈0.30° formulation, softened exclusivity language, fixed ZIP metadata, and set deterministic compilation date.
key_changes: Deterministic date: \today → 'February 28, 2026' + \paperVersion{v0.7.0} macros
TB consistency fix: removed incorrect C_ℓ^{TB}/C_ℓ^{TE} = 0.98±0.12 → cross-frequency stability across 100/143/217 GHz
Exclusivity language softened: 'uniquely provides...no other model' → 'distinctive combination' / 'To our knowledge'
ZIP README: San Francisco → Los Angeles, HUBIFY-2025-001 → HUBIFY-2026-001, 41 → 46 bib entries
index.html: 7σ→2.4–2.7σ, EB card updated to β≈0.30° formula, timeline 5σ→4.2–5.9σ
paper.html: abstract synced to main.tex, date to Feb 28, Table I row 5 JWST→birefringence
explained.html: 7σ→2.4–2.7σ with ACT caveats, EB description updated to all-ℓ formulation
datasets.html: 3× ~7σ replacements in honest assessment, contingency, and scorecard
methodology.html: EB amplitude formula updated, AI consensus marketing → proper disclaimer
Created version.json at repo root
PDF recompiled: 27 pages, 0 undefined references
versions.note: Canonical source of truth: arxiv/main.tex. All website pages now mirror LaTeX exactly.

versions.title: Nuclear Option — Maximum Credibility Revision
versions.description: Harsh Reviewer #2 response: removed all overclaims, deleted forecast section and Appendix H entirely, removed Ω_k from MCMC (92 e-folds kills it), softened all 'prediction' language to 'MCMC fits', added explicit order-of-magnitude gap for galaxy spin A_0, removed β=0.30° from abstract.
key_changes: Section IX (Detection Timeline) DELETED entirely
Appendix H (Forecast) DELETED entirely
Ω_k removed from MCMC: fixed to 0 (92 e-folds mandates this)
Abstract: 'deriving dark energy' → 'modeling dark energy as arising from'
All 'predictions' → 'MCMC fits' or 'testable outputs'
β=0.30° removed from abstract (no photon coupling to derive it)
Galaxy spin 37-order gap explicitly documented
A(z) functional form labeled 'phenomenological' throughout
All 'resolves' → 'partially reduces' for tensions
Parameter count: 8 → 7 (after Ω_k removal)
versions.note: 10 critical/major issues from comprehensive audit — all addressed. Option A: keep inflationary dilution, drop bounce relic claims.

versions.title: Reproducibility Bundle + Claim Downgrades
versions.description: Complete reproducibility overhaul (Route 2: Conservative). Created honest bundle with 4 working Cobaya v3.5 YAMLs using stock CAMB, Stan galaxy spin model, one-command reproduce scripts. Downgraded all claims depending on non-existent artifacts. Renamed 'Geometric Dilution Parameter' to 'Inflationary Suppression Factor'. Added Claims Classification Table (Appendix K). Website fully synced.
key_changes: 'Geometric Dilution Parameter' → 'Inflationary Suppression Factor' (14 instances + all HTML)
Affiliation: 'Independent Researcher, Los Angeles, California, USA'
MCMC section: 'modified CAMB v1.5' → 'stock CAMB (no custom modifications)'
CMB EB section: 'estimated from Planck HFI using SMICA' → 'drawn entirely from published literature'
Data & Code Availability: complete rewrite with honest artifact list
Appendix J (Reproducibility): rewritten to reference actual Cobaya YAMLs and Stan model
Galaxy spins reframed as 'Contested Anomaly' with null-result paragraph
JWST removed from Table II
Claims Classification Table added as Appendix K
Removed fictional camb_modifications.diff, cobaya_config.yaml, params_bestfit.ini
Created 4 working Cobaya YAMLs + reproduce scripts in reproducibility/
Created IMPLEMENTATION_MAP.md and KNOWN_GAPS.md
CNN classifier references removed; uses published catalog labels
All CMB null tests attributed to literature
PDF recompiled: 29 pages, 0 undefined references
versions.note: Route 2 (Conservative): every 'This work' number traceable to a working script or honest literature citation.

versions.title: v1.0 Final — Research Issues + New Citations + Full Site Sync
versions.description: Comprehensive research agent sweep (148 papers, 9 equation checks, 5 cross-checks) identified 6 substantive issues and 8 new citations. All issues addressed, 4 new BibTeX entries added (Yin et al. 2026, Diego-Palazuelos & Komatsu 2025, DESI DR2 2025, Sanyal et al. 2026), inline citations placed in relevant sections. Vacuum energy dilution mechanism clarified, σ₈ weak lensing framing strengthened, MCMC statistical equivalence disclosure added, N=92 e-fold breakdown specified. Full HTML site synced. PDF recompiled.
key_changes: A1: Clarified inflationary suppression sets primordial coefficient, not 'dilutes vacuum energy'
A2: Verified 4.9σ consistency throughout (no stale 4.4σ references found)
A3: Strengthened σ₈ tension framing: primary tension is CMB vs weak lensing (KiDS-1000, DES Y3)
A4: Added explicit MCMC = standard ΔNeff extension disclosure in Sec VII.B
A5: Added N=92 breakdown: ~55-60 observable + ~30 pre-observable e-folds
A6: Conjunctive falsification already present (verified, no changes needed)
Added Yin et al. 2026 (arXiv:2601.13624): birefringence + EDE joint constraints
Added Diego-Palazuelos & Komatsu 2025 (arXiv:2509.13654): ACT DR6 β=0.215°±0.074° (2.9σ)
Added DESI 2025 DR2 (arXiv:2503.14738): strengthened dynamical dark energy evidence
Added Sanyal et al. 2026 (arXiv:2602.15924): cosmic hysteresis in f(T) bounce
Updated conclusions with ACT DR6 independent confirmation and DESI DR2
All HTML pages synced with updated paper content
Version macros updated to v1.0.0, date to March 4, 2026
PDF recompiled with 0 undefined references
versions.note: v1.0 release: all research issues from comprehensive agent sweep addressed, 50 bibliography entries.

versions.title: v1.1 — S₈ Computation, CAMB Verification, 6 New Citations
versions.description: RunPod GPU infrastructure integration session. CAMB numerical verification confirmed paper σ₈ values. Computed and reported S₈ = 0.80 ± 0.02 for the spin-torsion model (reduces S₈ tension from ~3σ to ~1σ vs weak lensing). Added 6 new citations from comprehensive literature survey. Updated DESI DR2 significance to 3.1-4.2σ. Updated birefringence numbers throughout (ACT DR6 2.9σ, SPIDER combined 7σ). All 4 Cobaya MCMC chains running on RunPod GPU pod for independent verification.
key_changes: NEW: S₈ = 0.80 ± 0.02 computed and reported (Eq. 3, abstract, Table 4, conclusions)
NEW: S₈ tension quantified: ~1σ vs DES Y3, down from ~3σ in ΛCDM
CAMB numerical verification on RunPod: σ₈, S₈, Ωm confirmed at 5 parameter points
Added SPIDER+Planck+ACT combined 7σ rotation (arXiv:2510.25489)
Added EC torsion AIC-preferred by DESI DR2+Planck (arXiv:2507.04265)
Added TorC Hubble tension alleviation (arXiv:2507.09228)
Added Fabbri propagating torsion singularity avoidance (arXiv:2502.17979)
Added Shim et al. DESI galaxy spin 13σ forecast (arXiv:2406.06080)
Added ACT DR6 Neff = 2.86 ± 0.13 constraint (arXiv:2503.14452)
Updated DESI DR2: 2.5-3.9σ → 3.1-4.2σ throughout
Updated birefringence: 2.4-2.7σ → 2.4-2.9σ (including ACT DR6) throughout
All 4 Cobaya MCMC chains launched on RunPod (Planck, +BAO, +SN, +Full Tension)
Integration test suite: 15/15 passing on RunPod GPU pod
PDF recompiled: 32 pages, 57 references, 0 undefined

versions.title: v1.2 — Independent MCMC Verification, ACT DR6 Constraints, Model Comparison
versions.description: Comprehensive research update incorporating preliminary MCMC chain results from 4 independent Cobaya v3.6.1 chains, ACT DR6 Neff constraint analysis, Liu et al. EC torsion cross-validation, sound-horizon-free H₀ convergence at 69.37±0.34, model comparison scorecard against 6 competing frameworks, and 6 new citations. Paper updated to reflect dataset-dependent ΔNeff (0.1-0.5) and ACT DR6 as most stringent observational challenge.
key_changes: NEW: Independent MCMC verification results — ΔNeff dataset-dependent (0.46 CMB-only → 0.11 full)
NEW: ACT DR6 Neff = 2.86 ± 0.13 constraint analysis — most stringent challenge to framework
NEW: Liu et al. (2507.04265) cross-validation — all parameters agree within 1.6σ
NEW: Sound-horizon-free H₀ = 69.37 ± 0.34 from Pantos & Perivolaropoulos (2601.00650)
NEW: Model comparison scorecard — BigBounce vs ΛCDM, EDE, νDM, Liu EC, w0waCDM
NEW: Shamir JWST JADES galaxy spin asymmetry (2502.18781) — 65% CW in 2200 spirals
NEW: Alam et al. bouncing cosmologies in modified gravity with torsion (2509.03508)
Added ACT DR6 baseline H₀ = 68.22 ± 0.36 reference (2503.14452)
Updated H₀ comparison table with SHF consensus and ACT DR6 baseline
Updated ΔNeff range from 0.2-0.5 to 0.1-0.5 reflecting dataset dependence
6 new figures: parameter space, tension comparison, model comparison, σ₈/S₈, Neff, galaxy spin
